Why Siblings Sessions Pay Eligibility FAQ
THE ROLE OF SIBLINGS IN AI

Why your resemblance matters to AI

AI systems learn from real-world video and audio data. People who closely resemble each other provide something no other data source can.

Shared genetics, subtle differences

Identical siblings share genetics but develop unique vocal tones, expressions, and mannerisms. This controlled variation teaches AI to see what makes each person distinct.

Siblings who look alike

Same-gender biological siblings who closely resemble each other help AI understand how family resemblance works — a challenge current systems still struggle with.

Advancing responsible AI

Your recordings directly support the development and improvement of AI systems through responsible research and data collection practices.

Apply to This Project
WHAT YOU'LL DO

Your guided recording session

One 2–3 hour session of simple, guided activities. Everything is explained step-by-step before you begin.

Read Short Prompts

Read brief on-screen prompts aloud at your own pace. No memorization — just natural reading in your normal voice.

Speak Naturally

Talk in your own words on simple guided topics. No scripts, no acting — just your natural speaking voice and cadence.

Basic Facial Expressions

Make simple, natural facial expressions — smiling, neutral, surprised. No acting skills needed, just be yourself.

Everyday Movements

Complete simple, everyday movements and gestures as guided. Nothing physical or strenuous — just natural, routine actions.

Apply to This Project
SESSION DETAILS

What to expect

Your session (2–3 hours, one visit)

You'll complete one recorded session alongside your sibling. During the session you'll read short prompts, speak naturally, make basic facial expressions, and complete simple everyday movements.

Everything is guided step-by-step. All sessions are audio and video recorded for research purposes. Each participant may complete one session only.

Onsite vs. at home

Onsite: Visit a designated recording location in your city. Professional setup, controlled environment, trained facilitators on hand.

At home: Complete your session remotely through a coordinated in-home recording with guided support. Same compensation either way.

What to bring

Required: A valid government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, or state ID). Both participants must present ID.

Recommended: Wear solid-colored clothing and arrive with your hair as you normally wear it. No other preparation needed.

Day-of timeline

Arrival: Check in, present your photo ID, and review the informed consent form for audio and video recording.

Session: A trained facilitator guides you through each activity. Everything is explained before you begin. Breaks are built in.

After: Session complete. Payment is queued for the next twice-monthly payout. No follow-up obligations unless you choose to participate in future studies.

SAFETY & PRIVACY

Your data, your control

Informed consent required

You'll review and sign a consent form before any recording begins. Participation is entirely voluntary.

Responsible data practices

All recordings are collected through responsible research and data collection practices, and are never sold or shared publicly.

Audio & video recording

All sessions are recorded for research purposes. You'll know exactly what's being captured and why before you start.

ID verification

Both participants must present a valid government-issued photo ID. This protects you and the integrity of the research.

ELIGIBILITY

Is this right for you both?

Review the requirements below. If you meet all seven, you and your sibling are eligible.

18 years of age or older

Both participants must be at least 18.

Identical siblings or same-gender biological siblings who closely resemble each other

Siblings must be within 8 years of age difference.

Both must participate together

Both siblings must complete the session. One person alone cannot participate.

Able to follow instructions in English

Session activities are guided in English.

Willing to provide informed consent for audio and video recording

All sessions are recorded for research purposes. You'll review and sign a consent form.

Valid government-issued photo ID

Both participants must present a valid photo ID (driver's license, passport, or state ID).

Reside in an active U.S. recruitment city

This study is available in select U.S. cities. Click Apply to check if your city is currently active.

Not sure if you qualify? Apply anyway. Our team reviews every application individually and responds within 1–3 business days.

Apply to This Project
FAQ

Common questions

Do both siblings need to participate?

Yes. Both siblings must participate together in the same session. One person alone cannot take part. Each participant is compensated separately — $700 each for identical siblings.

How long is the session?

The session is 2–3 hours. You'll complete all activities in a single visit — reading short prompts, speaking naturally, making facial expressions, and completing simple movements. Each participant may complete one session only.

How and when will I be paid?

Twice monthly (1st and 15th) via Payoneer or PayPal. After your session is verified, payment is queued for the next cycle. Zero fees are ever charged to participants.

What will my recordings be used for?

Your recordings support the development and improvement of artificial intelligence systems. Data is collected through responsible research practices and is never sold or shared publicly.

Is this available outside the US?

This study is currently recruiting in select U.S. cities only. You must reside in one of the active recruitment cities to participate. Check eligibility when you apply.

Do siblings have to be the same age?

No, but siblings must be the same gender, biological, closely resemble each other, and be within 8 years of age difference. If you're unsure whether you qualify, apply — our team will assess eligibility with no penalty.

What do I need to bring?

A valid government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, or state ID). Wear solid-colored clothing and come with your hair as you normally wear it. No scripts, no special equipment — we handle everything else.

Is this a one-time commitment?

Yes. Each participant may complete one session only, with no follow-up obligations. Participation is voluntary. If you'd like to hear about future studies, you can opt in to notifications.
